_readme: Crop LIST for Pune district is grounded in the sources below — this part is trustworthy. Yield/price/cost figures per crop are NOT yet sourced (still placeholder-level, verified: false) — that's the next research pass. Channel access notes are qualitative judgments based on general knowledge of how each buyer type operates, not confirmed district-specific contracts — treat as a starting hypothesis to validate, not fact.
sources: https://indiandistricts.in/statistics/maharashtra/pune/agriculture/ — taluka-level crop specialties (Baramati/Haveli grapes, Junnar banana, Purandar fig/pomegranate)
https://www.mofpi.gov.in (ODOP state profile) — Pune district's One-District-One-Product is tomato-based products, confirming tomato + processing demand

channelTypes.mandi: APMC wholesale mandi — open auction, live price via Agmarknet, ~6-10% commission, no quality bar but no price floor either
channelTypes.b2b_aggregator: DeHaat / Ninjacart / WayCool style — aggregates from farmers for retail & food-service supply chains, wants volume + grading, price not public
channelTypes.quick_commerce: BigBasket/Blinkit/Zepto/Reliance Fresh style direct sourcing — realistic for Pune given urban proximity, wants consistent perishable quality, price not public
channelTypes.contract_farming: Pre-agreed/formula pricing with an input or processing company (e.g. Syngenta runs a Pune-based program) — reduces price-crash risk, often bundles inputs/advisory, less price upside
channelTypes.fpo_collective: Farmer-owned producer company/collective (Sahyadri Farms model, Nashik-based but same model applies) — pooled bargaining power, best where an active collective exists for that crop

crops.note: Baramati and Haveli talukas are known for grape cultivation
crops.channelNote: Export-oriented FPO model (Sahyadri-style) is proven for grapes in this region, though the flagship FPO itself is Nashik-based

crops.note: Listed among Pune's major horticultural crops
crops.channelNote: High price volatility crop nationally — good candidate to show risk clearly, not just upside

crops.note: Pune district's ODOP (One District One Product) is tomato-based processing, confirming both cultivation scale and processing demand
crops.channelNote: Processing-company contract farming is realistic here given the ODOP designation
Export potential exists nationally for pomegranate but needs district-specific verification

crops.note: Purandar taluka is specifically known for anjeer (fig) — niche, high-value crop
crops.channelNote: Niche/premium crop — smaller volumes, likely limited aggregator interest, worth checking local FPO presence
High-volume perishable — good fit for aggregator/quick-commerce channels generally

crops.note: Grown in western, higher-rainfall talukas (Mulshi, Bhor, Velhe) and irrigated tracts
crops.channelNote: Sugarcane is structurally almost always sold to a sugar factory under a season-long arrangement, not mandi or retail — this is the norm nationally, not Pune-specific
Staple grain, mandi is the dominant channel; MSP procurement may apply — needs verification
